name: Bhai Balkar Singh Multani
body: Bhai Balkar Singh Multani was born on April 4th, 1955, into the household of Sardar Singh Ji and Mata Kartar Kaur Ji, in Jaid Bassi village, district Kapurthala, Punjab. He was the second child among nine siblings.

His interest in music found its roots in familial influences, particularly through his paternal uncle, Giani Charan Singh Ji, a Granthi who performed melodious Kirtan within the local village Gurdwara. Additionally, his maternal uncle, Sardar Singh Dardi, was a Shiromani Kavishar and Dhadhi, further ignited Multani Ji’s passion for music.

Having completed his matriculation at Sant Prem Singh Secondary School, Multani Ji’s brother-in-law helped him to secure a job as a manager in a Gurdwara in Hanumangarh, Rajasthan. He was employed at this Gurdwara for approximately one year. However, driven by his deep-seated affinity for music, he proceeded to enroll in the Sikh Missionary College at Buddhajohar in Sri Ganganagar, Rajasthan, from 1974 to 1977. Under the tutelage of Prof. Pritam Singh Zakhmi, a revered figure in the field of Sikh music and a former teacher at the Central Khalsa Orphanage in Amritsar, Multani Ji acquired comprehensive musical training.

Subsequent to earning his Bachelor of Arts in Music, Multani Ji briefly engaged in Kirtan service in Ludhiana before relocating to Nakodar, where he spent a significant period from 1980 to 1983. It was during this time that he met Bhai Jasvir Singh, who later joined his ‘jatha’ after learning to play the tabla from Multani Ji. Moving back to Ludhiana, Multani Ji served as a Hazoori Raagi at Gurdwara Model Town, followed by a brief stint at Gurdwara Sri Kalgidhar Sahib. His next service was at Gurdwara Sri Guru Nanak Mission in Jalandhar, where he served as Hazoori Raagi from 1991 to 1995.

Multani Ji’s international tours began in 1989 with visits to Thailand and Malaysia, subsequently to Canada in 1994 and 1995. A more extensive tour spanning England, the United States of America, and Canada followed in 1996. Over the ensuing years, he undertook multiple trips to Canada between 1997 and 1999, and then to England again in 2000, where Bhai Jasvir Singh decided to remain and establish residence.

Multani Ji also served as a Hazoori Raagi at Gurdwara Shaheed Baba Deep Singh Ji in Amritsar for many years, and occasionally performed Kirtan at Sri Harmandir Sahib as well. Regrettably, Multani Ji had to resign due to health concerns, and passed away peacefully on February 1st, 2021. He is survived by his son and two daughters. One of Multani Ji’s brothers, Bhai Avtar Singh Ji, who had accompanied him on the side harmonium, continues to perform Kirtan and is carrying forward the tradition.
If anyone has more information or recordings of Bhai Balkar Singh Ji, please contact us.
The recordings below are from the Boston Sikh Sangat Gurdwara in 2006. These were obtained from the ‘Sikh Sangeet’ website.
